original_abstract: A simple and efficient iron-catalyzed method for the 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkynes and alkenes is reported. Utilizing inexpensive FeBr2 as the catalyst and Cs2CO3 as the base, the reaction proceeds under mild conditions to provide various perfluoroalkylated vicinal iodoalkanes and iodoalkenes in high yields with excellent functional group compatibility. Systematic screening revealed that while several transition metals facilitate the transformation, iron-based systems offer superior reactivity and selectivity. The resulting iodinated products serve as versatile synthetic intermediates, as demonstrated by their subsequent application in various cross-coupling reactions, including Suzuki, Sonogashira, and iron-catalyzed Grignard couplings, to access complex fluorinated organic molecules. This protocol provides a practical and sustainable pathway for the late-stage incorporation of perfluoroalkyl groups into unsaturated hydrocarbon frameworks.
humanized_abstract: A straightforward iron catalysis approach to 1,2-addition of perfluoroalkyl iodides to alkynes and alkenes was developed. Reaction with iron catalyst FeBr2 and base Cs2CO3 at room temperature gives title products of interest for further cross coupling reactions in high yields without any compatibility issue. A detailed catalyst search revealed that transition metals could catalyze the reaction, however iron catalysts were the most efficient for this reaction. The obtained iodinated products were subsequently cross-coupled using Suzuki, Sonogashira or iron catalysis Grignard reactions, giving highly fluorinated molecules.
